frameworks.name: noradrenergic modulation of memory encoding
frameworks.one_line: locus coeruleus noradrenaline preferentially enhances encoding of salient negative events

frameworks.name: MRI neuromelanin imaging
frameworks.one_line: neuromelanin MRI contrast indexes structural integrity of the locus coeruleus

frameworks.name: pupillometry as LC activation index
frameworks.one_line: pupil diameter used as putative readout of phasic LC activation

core_question: Does individual variation in locus coeruleus structural integrity in older adults relate to memory for emotionally salient events?
key_assumptions: neuromelanin MRI contrast reflects LC structural integrity
pupil diameter indexes LC activation during encoding
noradrenergic modulation preferentially affects salient/aversive event encoding
manipulations: emotional salience / valence of encoded events (negative vs neutral)
incidental memory encoding task susceptible to noradrenergic modulation
key_findings: LC structural integrity varies considerably across healthy older adults
older adults with reduced LC integrity show poorer subsequent memory
the integrity-memory effect is more pronounced for emotionally negative events
salient stimuli elicited greater pupil dilation during encoding
interpretations: LC integrity selectively supports memory for salient negative events in aging via noradrenergic modulation
pupil dilation to salient stimuli is consistent with increased LC activation during encoding
